summary: A 2D side-scrolling auto-battler where the player manages a moving castle by placing units into its rooms to attack an enemy castle while defending their own.
objective: Destroy the enemy's red castle by depleting its health to 0% before the player's blue castle is destroyed.

controls.observation: Drag and drop gesture to select a unit from the bottom UI panel and place it into an empty room in the player's castle.

layout.observation: Top of screen: Two health bars with percentage text. Blue on the left (player), Red on the right (enemy).
Center: Battlefield showing the two castles facing each other.
Bottom: Unit selection panel with three character options (Cyclops, Skeleton, Orc) appears when a room is available.

entities.name: Blue Castle
entities.visible_behavior: Moves slightly, takes damage, houses player units in specific room slots.

entities.name: Red Castle
entities.visible_behavior: Moves slightly, takes damage, houses enemy units that automatically fire at the player.

entities.name: Skeleton Unit
entities.visible_behavior: Placed in a room, automatically fires projectiles at the enemy castle.

entities.name: Orc Unit
entities.visible_behavior: Placed in a room, automatically fires projectiles at the enemy castle.

entities.name: Cyclops Unit
entities.visible_behavior: Available in the UI, but not placed during the video.

entities.name: Projectiles
entities.visible_behavior: Fired by units, travel across the screen, explode on impact with the opposing castle.

timeline.observation: Battle begins, castles exchange fire, health bars decrease.
Blue castle takes significant damage, revealing empty rooms. Unit selection UI appears.
Player drags the Skeleton unit into the top empty room.
Skeleton unit begins firing at the Red castle.
Another room becomes empty. Player drags the Orc unit into the bottom room.
Blue castle health reaches 0% and explodes.

mechanics.observation: Global health system for each castle, represented by the top bars.
Slot-based unit placement. Units can only be dropped into designated empty rooms.
Auto-attack: Once placed, units automatically spawn projectiles aimed at the enemy castle.
Win/Loss condition triggered when a castle's health reaches 0.

visual_requirements: Parallax scrolling or static background with moving elements to simulate tank tread movement.
Explosion and smoke particle effects when projectiles hit.
Visual degradation of castles as they take damage (e.g., walls breaking away).
UI overlays for health bars and unit selection.
prototype_must_have: Two castles with overall health bars.
Ability to drag and drop at least one unit type into predefined slots on the player's castle.
Units automatically firing projectiles at the enemy castle.
Projectiles dealing damage and reducing castle health.
A win/loss state when a health bar reaches 0.
prototype_can_skip: Complex visual degradation of the castles (walls breaking away dynamically).
Multiple unit types (one is sufficient for testing the core loop).
implementation_notes: Define invisible drop zones (hitboxes) within the Blue Castle sprite for unit placement.
Use a simple state machine for units: 'In UI', 'Being Dragged', 'Placed/Firing'.
Projectiles can be implemented with simple linear interpolation (lerp) or basic physics velocity towards the enemy castle's bounding box.
Castle health can be a single global variable per side, ignoring individual room health for the prototype.

uncertainties.question: Do individual units have their own health, or do they die based on the castle's overall damage state?
uncertainties.why_it_matters: Affects whether units need their own health tracking and hit detection, or if they are just visual representations of the castle's attack power.

uncertainties.question: Are the castles actually moving forward, or is the background scrolling to create the illusion of movement?
uncertainties.why_it_matters: Determines if the camera and physics need to handle moving objects or just a scrolling background.
